i mean i want to know if a person can do an infographic where they can handwrite or do handwriting

4:59 pm

That's a great question! Yes, absolutely — there are several ways to create an infographic with handwriting. Here are your main options:

By hand (traditional):

  • Draw and write everything on paper or a poster board, then photograph or scan it
  • Use markers, brush pens, or calligraphy pens for a polished look

Digitally with a stylus/tablet:

  • Apps like Procreate (iPad), Adobe Fresco, or Clip Studio Paint let you handwrite directly on a digital canvas
  • GoodNotes or Notability are great for a more natural handwritten feel
  • Canva also has a drawing/handwriting mode if you're using a tablet with a stylus

Hybrid approach:

  • Design the layout digitally in Canva or Adobe Express, then print it and handwrite the text over it
  • Or handwrite elements on paper, scan them, and layer them into a digital design

Fonts as a shortcut:

  • If you want the look of handwriting without actually writing, tools like Canva, Adobe Express, or even Google Slides have handwriting-style fonts you can type with

What I can do for you: I can build you an infographic (as an HTML or SVG file) that uses handwriting-style fonts to give it that personal, hand-crafted feel — it would look handwritten but be fully digital.

Would you like me to make one like that? If so, just tell me the topic!

here they are!! both files ready 🐾💜 here's what each one has:

file 1 — teaser-for-ruth.html 🐶 a snoopy-themed countdown page with a blue sky, clouds, little woodstock birds, a doghouse that says "ruth's", and a speech bubble that cycles through silly snoopy messages like "snoopy is personally wrapping this for you rn" — counting down to april 8, 2026!

file 2 — anniversary-app-ruth.html 🎉 the full silly snoopy/loopy app with everything — poem in comic panels, the letter with editable boxes, spotify playlist embedder + song list, and the full memory scrapbook with stickers, photos & captions. all lowercase, all cute, all chaotic 🐤

how to share it with her 💜

since these are html files, here are your options:

  1. send the file directly — just send her the .html file over messenger, discord, telegram, etc. she opens it in her phone or browser and it works instantly. no internet needed!
  2. upload to a free website — go to tiiny.host or netlify drop, drag and drop the file, and it gives you a link you can send her like a normal message 🔗 this is the cutest way to do it!
  3. google drive — upload it to your drive, right-click → share → anyone with link, then send her the link. she opens it and taps the preview!

option 2 (tiiny.host or netlify) is the easiest and most romantic — she just gets a mystery link 🐾💜

Created a file, read a file

here it is!! 🩺💜 here's everything inside ruth's study corner:

🃏 flashcards

  • preset FEU BSN decks by year — pulled straight from the actual FEU 2021 curriculum:
    • 🔴 1st year — anatomy & physiology, theoretical foundations, retorika, health assessment
    • 🟡 2nd year — fundamentals of nursing, pharmacology, community health nursing I, care of mother & child
    • 🟢 3rd year — mother & child at-risk, oxygenation & immunologic problems, community health II
    • 🟣 4th year — psychiatric nursing, critical care & emergency, leadership & management, disaster nursing
  • ✨ ai flashcard generator — she types any topic (e.g. "cardiac meds", "respiratory acidosis") and it auto-generates cards using ai, specific to the Philippine NLE
  • 🛠️ custom deck builder — she can build her own decks card by card and save them
  • flip animation on cards, progress bar, add cards to any deck

📓 notes

  • create color-coded notebooks
  • write, edit, search, and pin notes
  • auto-saves as she types
  • lined paper background for that notebook feel

📋 to-do list — with school / personal / reminder / urgent categories

🔔 reminders — with date & time, shows overdue alerts in red

and yes — no snoopy drawings from me this time 😄 you can drop the snoopy images in yourself! just let me know if you want a spot in the main app to link to this study file 💜

both files are updated!! here's everything that's new 🐾💜

teaser — new games:

study corner — big upgrades:

flashcards now have 5 quiz modes — switch between them at the top of any open deck:

  • 🃏 flashcards — original flip mode
  • 🔵 multiple choice — 4 choices, auto-generated wrong answers, shows score at end
  • ✅ true or false — answers presented as statements, tap true or false
  • ✏️ identification — type the answer yourself, checks if you're close enough
  • 🔗 matching — tap a question then tap its answer to match pairs

ai generator now has 3 modes:

  • 📝 by topic — type any nursing topic
  • 📄 from pdf — upload your actual pdf notes, the app reads them and generates cards from the real content
  • ✍️ manual text — paste any text and it makes cards from it

notes — full rich text editor:

  • bold, italic, underline, strikethrough
  • bullet lists & numbered lists
  • heading style
  • color backgrounds per note
  • auto-saves everything as she types 💜

6:58 pm

for the polaroids — they're already in both files! here's exactly where to find them:

teaser → tap the 📸 photos tab in the bottom nav → you'll see 6 polaroid frames → tap any one to upload your photos from your camera roll 💜

main app → scroll to the hero section (top of the page) → there are 6 polaroid frames right below the "happy anniversary" panel → tap any to upload

for the iphone emojis — i can't actually swap the system emojis since those depend on the device 😅 but here's the thing: if ruth opens the app on her iphone or ipad, she'll automatically see all the emojis in apple's style!! 🍎 because iphones render all emojis using apple's emoji font natively — so she doesn't need to do anything, it'll already look like iphone emojis on her end 💜

the only time they look different is if YOU are viewing it on android or a non-apple browser — but since she uses an iphone and ipad, it'll be perfect for her!!

the crop CSS was injected into the file but using classes that aren't in the style block — it's using inline styles in the HTML. let me now do a full fix of the crop modal (smaller, more accessible), fix the photo change/delete in both files, and reduce lag in the main app:

all fixed!! 🐾💜 here's what changed:

teaser-v8.html — crop & photo fixes:

  • crop modal is now smaller — only 320px wide, 180px tall preview, fits properly on iphone screen without being overwhelming
  • × close button in the top right of the crop modal so she can dismiss it easily
  • cancel/save buttons are full-width at the bottom, easy to tap on mobile
  • delete × button on photos is now smaller (16px) and sits cleanly in the corner without overlapping the caption

for-ruth-v5.html — lag fixes:

  • image compression — photos are now automatically resized to max 800×600px and compressed to 75% quality before saving. this massively reduces the localStorage size and makes everything faster
  • polaroid uploads compressed to 600×480px too
  • orbs pause when you switch tabs or lock your phone — no wasted processing in the background
  • mobile orb optimization — on phones, the blur is reduced from 90px to 60px (blur is one of the most expensive CSS effects) and the grain texture is hidden on small screens
  • will-change hint on animated elements so the browser pre-optimizes them
  • same small × delete button fix as the teaser

inetifly isnt loading fast do you have other alternatives?

8:35 pm

yes!! here are better alternatives 🐾

🥇 best option — github pages (free, fast, permanent)

  1. go to github.com → sign up free
  2. click "new repository" → name it anything (e.g. for-ruth)
  3. make sure it's set to public
  4. click "uploading an existing file" → drag both html files in
  5. go to settings → pages → under "branch" select main → click save
  6. your links will be:
    • yourusername.github.io/for-ruth/teaser-v8.html
    • yourusername.github.io/for-ruth/for-ruth-v5.html
  7. takes about 1–2 minutes to go live, then it's fast forever 💜

🥈 tiiny.host (easiest, no account needed)

  1. go to tiiny.host on your phone or computer
  2. tap "upload" → pick your html file
  3. give it a name (e.g. for-ruth-anniv)
  4. get your link instantly — something like for-ruth-anniv.tiiny.site
  5. free tier keeps it live for 48 hours — upgrade ($5) for permanent

🥉 vercel (fast + free forever)

  1. go to vercel.com → sign up with github
  2. click "add new project" → choose your github repo
  3. it deploys automatically, super fast CDN
  4. your link: your-repo.vercel.app

📱 easiest for phone only — use files.fm

  1. go to files.fm on your phone
  2. upload the html file
  3. it gives you a direct link — she can open it in safari and add to home screen

my recommendation: github pages — it's free forever, fast worldwide, and the link never expires 💜 if you want i can walk you through it step by step!

ialready uploaded the song why isnt it playing even though i alreadyy tapped it

8:39 pm

the issue is that browsers block audio from playing until the user interacts with the page first — and the song file might be too large to save in localStorage. let me fix this properly 🐾

Ran 3 commands, viewed a file

also update the music button UI to be clearer:

Ran 4 commands, viewed a file, read a file

here's what was wrong and what's fixed 🎵

why the song wasn't playing — 3 reasons:

  1. file too large for localStorage — mp3 files are usually 3–8MB but localStorage only allows 5MB total. saving it as base64 makes it even bigger (~30% larger). it was silently failing and the song data was getting lost 💀
  2. browser autoplay policy — browsers block audio from auto-playing unless the user directly taps something. the old code tried to play on page load which gets blocked
  3. audio src not set properly — if localStorage failed to save, the src was empty

what's fixed:

  • now uses blob URL (URL.createObjectURL) instead of base64 — no size limit, loads instantly, works with any file size
  • plays immediately when you upload (the file picker tap counts as a user gesture so autoplay is allowed)
  • the music bar is now centered at the bottom with a clear 📁 song upload button and a separate play/pause button
  • shows the song name once loaded
  • if autoplay still gets blocked it shows ▶️ and you just tap play once manually

⚠️ important note about the song: because it uses a blob URL (not saved to localStorage), she'll need to re-upload the song each time she reopens the app from scratch. this is a browser limitation for audio files. the song name is saved so she'll know which file to pick again. if you want the song to always be there, the best option is to host the mp3 file on github alongside the html file and reference it directly — want me to set that up? 💜

on your phone (iphone/ipad):

on a computer:

  2. right-click the link → "save link as" or just click it and it opens
  3. press Ctrl+S (Windows) or Cmd+S (Mac) to save it
  4. save it to your Desktop so it's easy to find

then upload to github:

  1. go to github.com → sign up or log in
  2. tap the + button (top right) → "new repository"
  3. name it for-ruth → set to public → click "create repository"
  4. click "uploading an existing file"
  5. drag both html files onto the page (or tap to browse and select them)
  6. scroll down → click "commit changes"

  8. wait 1–2 minutes → your links will be:
yourusername.github.io/for-ruth/teaser-v9.html
yourusername.github.io/for-ruth/for-ruth-v5.html

autoplay blocked: NotSupportedError: Failed to load because no supported source was found.

this is what it said when i tried to upload the sog

8:46 pm

that error means the audio format isn't supported by the browser! here's the fix 🐾

Ran 3 commands, read a file

here's what was wrong and what to do 🎵

the problem — your song file format

NotSupportedError: no supported source was found means the browser can't play that specific audio file format. this happens with:

  • .flac files
  • .aac files (sometimes)
  • some .wav files
  • any DRM-protected files (like songs downloaded from spotify/apple music)

what format WILL work:

  • ✅ mp3 — works on everything
  • ✅ m4a — works on iphone/safari
  • ✅ wav — works on most browsers
  • ❌ flac, ogg, protected aac — won't work in browser

how to get the right format for the Hozier cover:

option 1 — youtube to mp3 (easiest)

  1. find the Hozier "Do I Wanna Know" cover on youtube
  2. go to yt1s.com or ytmp3.cc
  3. paste the youtube link → download as mp3
  4. upload that mp3 to the teaser

option 2 — if you have it on spotify spotify songs are DRM protected so you can't directly use them — use the youtube method above instead

option 3 — use a file converter if you have the song in another format, go to cloudconvert.com → convert it to mp3 → upload that

the updated teaser-v10.html now also shows a warning message if the format isn't supported, so you'll know immediately instead of getting a confusing error 💜

Code · HTML 
2 / 2

do i just upload oth files in github at th same time? can youu give me stepby step procersss

8:50 pm

yes upload both at the same time!! here's the full step by step 🐾💜

step 1 — create a github account

  1. go to github.com on your phone or computer
  2. tap "sign up"
  3. enter your email, create a password, choose a username
  4. verify your email

step 2 — create a new repository (this is like a folder)

  1. after logging in, tap the + icon at the top right
  2. tap "new repository"
  3. under "repository name" type: for-ruth
  4. make sure "public" is selected (must be public for free hosting)
  5. scroll down and tap "create repository"

step 3 — upload your files

  1. on the next page you'll see your empty repo
  2. tap "uploading an existing file"
  3. a box will appear — drag both files at the same time into it:
    • teaser-v10.html
    • for-ruth-v5.html
    • your mp3 song file too if you have it!!
  4. wait for all files to show as uploaded ✅
  5. scroll down to where it says "commit changes"
  6. just tap the green "commit changes" button (don't change anything)

step 4 — turn on github pages (makes it a real website)

  1. tap "Settings" tab at the top of your repo
  2. scroll down the left sidebar and tap "Pages"
  3. under "Branch" tap the dropdown that says "None"
  4. select "main"
  5. tap "Save"
  6. wait 1–2 minutes ⏳

step 5 — get your links after waiting, refresh the pages settings and you'll see a box that says:

✅ your site is live at https://yourusername.github.io/for-ruth/

your two links will be:

  • https://yourusername.github.io/for-ruth/teaser-v10.html ← send this to her now as the teaser
  • https://yourusername.github.io/for-ruth/for-ruth-v5.html ← send this on april 8

step 6 — tell me your github username once you've uploaded the mp3 alongside the files, tell me your github username and the exact mp3 filename — i'll update the teaser so the song plays automatically without needing to upload it every time 💜

if you want to update the files later:

  1. go back to your repo on github
  2. click the file you want to replace
  3. tap the pencil ✏️ icon → then tap "delete file" OR
  4. just re-upload the new version and it replaces the old one

i cant find the oages part

8:52 pm

no worries!! here's exactly where to find it with more detail 🐾

finding github pages — step by step:

1. go to your repository (github.com/yourusername/for-ruth)

2. look at the row of tabs near the top of the page:

<> Code    Issues    Pull requests    Actions    Projects    Security    Settings

tap Settings (the last one with a ⚙️ gear icon)

3. on the Settings page, look at the left sidebar — you'll see a list like:

General
Access
  Collaborators
Code and automation
  Branches
  Tags
  Rules
  Actions
  Webhooks
  Environments
  Codespaces
  Pages        ← THIS ONE
Security
  ...

scroll down the left sidebar until you see Pages and tap it

4. on the Pages screen you'll see:

"Build and deployment"

  • Source: Deploy from a branch
  • Branch: None ▼ ← tap this dropdown
  • select main
  • tap Save

it still cant open it it said 404 File not found The site configured at this address does not contain the requested file. If this is your site, make sure that the filename case matches the URL as well as any file permissions.

8:57 pm

no worries!! this is a common issue. let me help you fix it 🐾

the most common reasons for 404:

reason 1 — pages not fully activated yet just wait 2-3 more minutes and try again. sometimes it takes a bit longer!

reason 2 — wrong filename in the URL the filename in the URL must match EXACTLY including capitals and dashes. check your repo:

  1. go to github.com/yourusername/for-ruth
  2. look at the files listed there
  3. what do the filenames say EXACTLY?

for example if the file is saved as For-Ruth-V5.html (capital letters) the link won't work with for-ruth-v5.html

reason 3 — files uploaded to a subfolder sometimes github puts files inside a folder. check if your files are directly in the main repo or inside a folder

quickest way to check and fix:

  1. go to your repo main page github.com/yourusername/for-ruth
  2. tap on one of the html files (like teaser-v10.html)
  3. at the top you'll see a button that says "Raw" — tap it
  4. copy that URL from your browser — that's the raw file link
  5. but instead of using that, take note of the EXACT filename shown

when you tap to download, before saving make sure the filename shows correctly. on iphone:

  1. tap the file link
  2. it opens in browser
  3. tap share ↑ → "save to files"
  4. before saving, tap the filename at the top and rename it to exactly:
    • teaser-v10.html
    • for-ruth-v5.html
  5. save it

step 2 — delete the wrong files on github

  2. tap Creating an infographic - Claude.html

  4. tap "commit changes"

step 3 — re-upload the correctly named files

  1. go back to your repo main page
  2. tap "Add files" → "Upload files"
  3. upload both correctly named html files
  4. tap "Commit changes"

https://archellearon-dev.github.io/for-ruth/for-ruth-v5.html

9:04 pm

yes!! the files NEED to be renamed exactly right or the links won't work 🐾

here's the easiest way to rename on iphone:

renaming on iphone — files app:

  1. open the Files app on your iphone
  2. find the downloaded files (check "Downloads" or "On My iPhone")
  3. long press on Creating an infographic - Claude.html
  4. tap "Rename"
  5. type exactly: teaser-v10.html
  6. tap done
  7. long press on uth anniv.html
  8. tap "Rename"
  9. type exactly: for-ruth-v5.html
  10. tap done

then re-upload to github:

  1. go to github.com/archellearon-dev/for-ruth
  2. tap "Add files" → "Upload files"
  3. upload both renamed files
  4. tap "Commit changes"

then your links will work:

https://archellearon-dev.github.io/for-ruth/teaser-v10.html

https://archellearon-dev.github.io/for-ruth/for-ruth-v5.html

how do i delete the previous ones? and where can i reupload

9:06 pm

here's exactly how 🐾

deleting the wrong files:

  1. go to github.com/archellearon-dev/for-ruth
  2. tap Creating an infographic - Claude.html
  3. look for the three dots menu ··· (top right of the file) OR a trash 🗑️ icon
  4. tap "Delete file"
  5. scroll down → tap "Commit changes" (green button)
  6. tap the back arrow to go back to the repo
  7. repeat for uth anniv.html — tap it → delete → commit

re-uploading the renamed files:

  1. go back to github.com/archellearon-dev/for-ruth
  2. tap the "Add files" button (top right area)
  3. tap "Upload files"
  4. tap "choose your files" or drag them in
  5. select both renamed files:
    • teaser-v10.html
    • for-ruth-v5.html
  6. scroll down → tap "Commit changes"

after that your links are:

archellearon-dev.github.io/for-ruth/teaser-v10.html

archellearon-dev.github.io/for-ruth/for-ruth-v5.html
